root_cause.text: The application previously did not track when a password was changed relative to the authentication time, allowing existing HTTP sessions to remain valid even after a password change.
root_cause.evidence: The addition of the `SessionResetFilter` class, which checks `hasPasswordChangedAfterAuthentication(lastAuthTime, passwordModTime)` and calls `session.invalidate()` if the password was changed after the session was authenticated.

vuln_statements.content: public static final String UPDATE_USER_SQL = "update users set version=?, lastModified=?, userName=?, email=?, givenName=?, familyName=?, active=?, phoneNumber=?, verified=?, origin=?, external_id=?, salt=?, passwd_lastmodified=? where id=? and version=?";

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The application previously did not track when a password was changed relative to the authentication time, allowing existing HTTP sessions to remain valid even after a password change.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The addition of the `SessionResetFilter` class, which checks `hasPasswordChangedAfterAuthentication(lastAuthTime, passwordModTime)` and calls `session.invalidate()` if the password was changed after the session was authenticated.

intent_record.intent_summary: Simplify HashSet instantiation using the diamond operator.
intent_record.what: Replaced the explicit type argument with a diamond operator in a HashSet instantiation.
intent_record.how: Simplifies the syntax without changing the runtime behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Introduce a filter to invalidate active sessions and force re-authentication if a user's password is changed post-authentication.
intent_record.what: Added a new SessionResetFilter class that intercepts requests to compare the authenticated user's last authentication time with their password's last modified time.
intent_record.how: Invalidates the HTTP session and redirects the user if their password was changed after they authenticated, or if the user no longer exists in the database.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add import for the Jackson JsonIgnore annotation.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnore.
intent_record.how: Makes the JsonIgnore annotation available in this class to prevent specific fields from being serialized or deserialized.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add authenticatedTime field to track authentication timestamp.
intent_record.what: Added a new member variable authenticatedTime to the class.
intent_record.how: Introduces state to store a timestamp, enabling the system to track when the authentication was established.

intent_record.intent_summary: Pass the current system time to the delegated UaaAuthentication constructor.
intent_record.what: Added System.currentTimeMillis() as an additional argument to the delegated constructor call.
intent_record.how: Ensures the UaaAuthentication object captures the exact time it was instantiated.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add authenticatedTime parameter to the UaaAuthentication constructor to support deserializing the authentication time.
intent_record.what: Added an 'authenticatedTime' parameter with a default value of '-1' to the constructor of UaaAuthentication.
intent_record.how: Enables the capture and deserialization of the 'authenticatedTime' property from JSON payloads into the UaaAuthentication object.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add authenticated time tracking and exclude the principal name from JSON serialization.
intent_record.what: Adds initialization and a getter for authenticatedTime, and applies the @JsonIgnore annotation to the getName() method.
intent_record.how: Initializes the authenticatedTime property with a default of -1 if it is 0, exposes it via a getter, and prevents the getName() method from being included in JSON serialization.

intent_record.intent_summary: Reformat UaaAuthentication instantiation for better readability.
intent_record.what: Reformatted the instantiation of UaaAuthentication to span multiple lines.
intent_record.how: This change has no effect on program behavior as it only alters whitespace for readability.

intent_record.intent_summary: Preserve user authentication time during identity zone switching.
intent_record.what: Added the authenticated time parameter to an authentication object instantiation.
intent_record.how: Ensures the original authentication time is preserved when creating the new authentication object during zone switching.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add unit tests for SessionResetFilter.
intent_record.what: Add a new file `SessionResetFilterTests.java` containing unit tests for `SessionResetFilter`.
intent_record.how: Provides test coverage for various authentication and session reset scenarios in `SessionResetFilter`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add tests to verify JSON deserialization behavior of the authenticatedTime field in UaaAuthentication.
intent_record.what: Added a new test class UaaAuthenticationSerializationTests with a test case for JSON deserialization of UaaAuthentication.
intent_record.how: Verifies that the authenticatedTime field is correctly parsed from JSON if present, and defaults to -1 if absent, ensuring expected deserialization behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports for UaaAuthentication and UaaAuthenticationDetails.
intent_record.what: Added imports for UaaAuthentication and UaaAuthenticationDetails classes.
intent_record.how: Resolves compilation dependencies by making the authentication classes available for use in the package.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add Java utility imports for Arrays and LinkedList.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for java.util.Arrays and java.util.LinkedList.
intent_record.how: Makes the Arrays and LinkedList classes available for use in the current Java file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update UaaAuthentication details after session invalidation during password change to prevent stale session references.
intent_record.what: Adds logic to instantiate a new UaaAuthentication object with updated UaaAuthenticationDetails based on the current request, replacing the existing authentication object if it is of type UaaAuthentication.
intent_record.how: Updates the authentication details to bind to the newly created session, ensuring the security context holds a valid and current session reference after a password change.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports for Calendar and GregorianCalendar.
intent_record.what: Added imports for Java Calendar classes.
intent_record.how: Allows the file to reference Calendar and GregorianCalendar classes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove password last modified field from the general user update SQL query to prevent unintended timestamp changes.
intent_record.what: Removed the `passwd_lastmodified=?` assignment from the `UPDATE_USER_SQL` query string.
intent_record.how: Prevents the application from updating the password last modified timestamp when performing general user profile updates.

intent_record.intent_summary: Process the timestamp through getPasswordLastModifiedTimestamp to ensure the correct value is persisted.
intent_record.what: Replaced the direct variable 't' with a call to 'getPasswordLastModifiedTimestamp(t)' when setting the timestamp parameter.
intent_record.how: Ensures that the password's last modified timestamp is properly processed or formatted before being saved to the database.

intent_record.intent_summary: Added a method to generate a current timestamp with milliseconds truncated.
intent_record.what: Added the getPasswordLastModifiedTimestamp method.
intent_record.how: Provides a way to obtain a current Timestamp with milliseconds truncated to zero.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove incorrect timestamp parameter assignment from PreparedStatement.
intent_record.what: Removed a statement setting a timestamp parameter in a PreparedStatement.
intent_record.how: Prevents assigning the timestamp value, adjusting the parameter indices to match the updated SQL query execution.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix the password last modified timestamp to use the correct calculation method.
intent_record.what: Changed the third parameter of a prepared statement to use getPasswordLastModifiedTimestamp(t) instead of the raw timestamp t.
intent_record.how: Ensures the password last modified timestamp is calculated correctly instead of just using the exact current timestamp.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix test assertion to ignore millisecond precision differences in timestamps.
intent_record.what: Modified an assertion comparing two Date objects to instead compare their epoch times truncated to the nearest second.
intent_record.how: Prevents test failures caused by millisecond precision differences that often occur when timestamps are persisted to and retrieved from a database.

intent_record.intent_summary: Truncate timestamp comparisons to the nearest second to fix flaky tests caused by database precision limitations.
intent_record.what: The assertions comparing 'Created' and 'LastModified' times with 'PasswordLastModified' times were updated to compare their epoch time values truncated to the nearest second.
intent_record.how: By dividing and then multiplying the timestamp values by 1000L, sub-second precision is removed before comparison, preventing assertion failures that happen due to slight millisecond differences between application and database timestamps.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add sessionResetFilter to the security filter chain to enforce session management policies.
intent_record.what: Added an entry for sessionResetFilter at position 102 in the security filter chain configuration.
intent_record.how: Incorporates a session reset filter into the application's filter chain, ensuring it executes at a specific point in the request processing lifecycle.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add sessionResetFilter bean definition to handle session resets.
intent_record.what: Added a new bean definition for sessionResetFilter with its required constructor arguments.
intent_record.how: Registers the SessionResetFilter in the Spring application context, allowing it to be used in the security filter chain to handle session resets and redirect to /login.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused import statements to clean up the code.
intent_record.what: Removed several unused import statements including SecureRandom and various JUnit assertions.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on runtime behavior as it only removes unused dependencies from the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add necessary imports for test assertions and random generation.
intent_record.what: Added imports for SecureRandom and several static assertion methods from JUnit and Hamcrest.
intent_record.how: Resolves compilation errors by making the required classes and assertion methods available to the test class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add static imports for MediaType.TEXT_HTML and MockMvcRequestBuilders.get for use in test cases.
intent_record.what: Added static imports for org.springframework.http.MediaType.TEXT_HTML and org.springframework.test.web.servlet.request.MockMvcRequestBuilders.get.
intent_record.how: Allows the test class to use the TEXT_HTML constant and the get() method directly without needing to fully qualify their class names.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add test to verify that password changes correctly invalidate all existing user sessions.
intent_record.what: Added a new test method named changePassword_Resets_All_Sessions.
intent_record.how: Verifies that when a password is changed, all previous sessions for that user are invalidated and redirected to the login page.

grouping_result.intent: Simplify HashSet instantiation using the diamond operator.
Add sessionResetFilter to the security filter chain to enforce session management policies.
Add authenticated time tracking and exclude the principal name from JSON serialization.
Add tests to verify JSON deserialization behavior of the authenticatedTime field in UaaAuthentication.
Pass the current system time to the delegated UaaAuthentication constructor.
Reformat UaaAuthentication instantiation for better readability.
Preserve user authentication time during identity zone switching.
Add necessary imports for test assertions and random generation.
Add imports for UaaAuthentication and UaaAuthenticationDetails.
Add Java utility imports for Arrays and LinkedList.
Update UaaAuthentication details after session invalidation during password change to prevent stale session references.
Add imports for Calendar and GregorianCalendar.
Remove incorrect timestamp parameter assignment from PreparedStatement.
Fix the password last modified timestamp to use the correct calculation method.
Added a method to generate a current timestamp with milliseconds truncated.
Truncate timestamp comparisons to the nearest second to fix flaky tests caused by database precision limitations.
Add sessionResetFilter bean definition to handle session resets.
Remove unused import statements to clean up the code.
Add static imports for MediaType.TEXT_HTML and MockMvcRequestBuilders.get for use in test cases.
Add test to verify that password changes correctly invalidate all existing user sessions.

consistency_review.core_intent: Implement and register a session reset filter to enforce session invalidation policies.
Add authenticated time tracking and configure JSON serialization for the principal.
Add the authenticatedTime field to UaaAuthentication, including its constructor parameter and corresponding deserialization tests.
Add test class and necessary test imports for SessionResetFilter
Remove unintended password last modified timestamp updates from the user update query and its PreparedStatement bindings.
Fix the calculation and persistence of the password last modified timestamp.
Fix flaky tests by truncating timestamp comparisons to the nearest second to ignore millisecond precision differences.

relevance_review.reason: The group only contains a refactoring change to use the diamond operator for a HashSet, which has no connection to tracking password change times or session validation.

The group introduces and registers a filter specifically designed to invalidate active sessions and force re-authentication after a password change, directly addressing the vulnerability's root cause.
Adding authenticated time tracking is directly required to compare the authentication time with the password change time, addressing the root cause.

The group adds the authenticatedTime field to the authentication context, directly addressing the vulnerability's root cause which states the application previously did not track the authentication time.

Passing the current system time to the UaaAuthentication constructor directly supports tracking the authentication time, which is essential to mitigating the vulnerability caused by not validating sessions after a password change.
The group only reformats code for better readability and does not address the logic related to tracking password changes or invalidating HTTP sessions.

Preserving the user authentication time is necessary to correctly implement the fix, as the vulnerability requires comparing the authentication time against the password change time to invalidate outdated sessions.
The group adds unit tests for the SessionResetFilter, which is the component responsible for mitigating the vulnerability by managing session validity after a password change.
The group only adds import statements, which do not directly contain the logic to address the vulnerability.
Adding utility imports for Arrays and LinkedList is a general code change and does not directly address the logic of tracking password changes or invalidating HTTP sessions.

The group's intent to update authentication details and invalidate sessions during a password change directly addresses the vulnerability where existing sessions remained valid after a password was changed.
Adding standard library imports is a boilerplate change and does not directly contain the logic to address the vulnerability.

The group addresses a bug in the newly introduced password last modified timestamp tracking by ensuring the timestamp is not inadvertently updated during a general user update, directly supporting the vulnerability fix.
Fixing the calculation and persistence of the password last modified timestamp directly addresses the root cause of tracking when a password was changed relative to authentication.

The utility method for generating a truncated timestamp is directly related to accurately tracking and comparing the password change time and authentication time, which is the core mechanism of the vulnerability fix.

The group focuses on fixing test flakiness by adjusting timestamp precision comparisons, which does not directly address the vulnerability's root cause of session invalidation after a password change.

Adding a sessionResetFilter bean directly addresses the root cause by providing a mechanism to reset/invalidate sessions, which is necessary to ensure existing sessions do not remain valid after a password change.
The group only removes unused import statements for code cleanup, which is a refactoring change completely unrelated to the vulnerability involving password changes and session invalidation.
The group only adds static imports for test utilities, which is unrelated to tracking password changes or invalidating HTTP sessions.
The group adds a test specifically designed to verify that password changes invalidate existing sessions, which directly addresses the root cause of the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: UaaAuthentication did not track the authentication time, calling the delegate constructor without a timestamp.
UaaAuthentication creation during zone switching did not preserve or include the authentication time.

candidates.content: public static final String UPDATE_USER_SQL = "update users set version=?, lastModified=?, userName=?, email=?, givenName=?, familyName=?, active=?, phoneNumber=?, verified=?, origin=?, external_id=?, salt=?, passwd_lastmodified=? where id=? and version=?";
candidates.rationale: The application unintentionally updated the password last modified time during general user updates, breaking the reference time for session invalidation.
Set the wrong precision/calculation for password last modified timestamp.

vuln_statements.explanation: The authentication object did not track the authentication time, calling the delegate constructor without a timestamp, which is required to compare against the password last modified time.
During identity zone switching, the new UaaAuthentication was created without preserving the original authentication time.

vuln_statements.stmt_text: public static final String UPDATE_USER_SQL = "update users set version=?, lastModified=?, userName=?, email=?, givenName=?, familyName=?, active=?, phoneNumber=?, verified=?, origin=?, external_id=?, salt=?, passwd_lastmodified=? where id=? and version=?";
vuln_statements.explanation: The general update query incorrectly updated the password last modified timestamp on every user update, rather than only when the password was actually changed.

zones.<id>.admin, zones.<id>.clients.admin, zones.<id>.clients.read, zones.<id>.cl

bic_trace_step.reason: The UaaAuthentication class does not include any mechanism to track the authentication time (e.g., an 'authenticatedAt' timestamp) to compare against a password change timestamp. The code only tracks 'principal', 'credentials', 'authorities', 'details', and 'authenticated' flag, leaving the vulnerability unmitigated.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 8d4afbc91b651e18fdb23fa8e83d68eb4166d915
Author: Filip Hanik <fhanik@gopivotal.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 14 08:01:45 2014 -0600

    Formatting
    Organize import
    Cleanup
    Copyright

diff --git a/common/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/authentication/UaaAuthentication.java b/common/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/authentication/UaaAuthentication.java
index c0c050704..999530041 100644
--- a/common/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/authentication/UaaAuthe
bic_trace_step.reason: The UaaAuthentication class represents an authenticated user session but lacks any fields or methods to track the time of authentication (e.g., authenticatedTime), which is the root cause of the vulnerability. This commit only changes formatting and copyright headers.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Initial draft - uaa with hard-coded user database
bic_trace_step.reason: The UaaAuthentication class only stores 'authorities' and 'principal'. It does not track the authentication time, which is the root cause of the vulnerability where existing HTTP sessions remain valid after a password change.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
The bootstrapped identity client can be used two ways:
- via client_credentials, it can create identity zones and create
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 6a3e64a95da72eb0c3766a9267d3fcd51b545172
Author: Will Tran <wtran@pivotal.io>
Date:   Sat Dec 6 12:28:50 2014 -0800

    The bootstrapped identity client can be used two ways:
    - via client_credentials, it can create identity zones and create
    clients in any identity zone
    - on behalf of a user, it can clients.(read|write|admin) if a user is
    part of those groups.
    
    If a user has scope zones.<zone_id>.admin, and the user's request has a
    header of X-Identity-Zone-Id:
bic_trace_step.reason: The provided code introduces 'IdentityZoneSwitchingFilter' to handle identity zone switching via the X-Identity-Zone-Id header. It does not handle password change tracking or HTTP session invalidation upon password change, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.

diff --git a/scim/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/scim/jdbc/JdbcScimUserProvisioning.java b/scim/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/scim/jdbc/JdbcScimUserProvisioning.java

bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces tracking of the 'passwd_lastmodified' field in the database during user creation, updates, and password changes (e.g., adding `passwd_lastmodified` to `CHANGE_PASSWORD_SQL`). This is part of the fix to track when a password was changed.
